mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or stays low, discount rates on future cash flows remain compressed and equity valuation multiples expand. That effect should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL trend over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when PAYEMS is also contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal vanishes when PAYEMS is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, which should support near-term corporate revenue expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-median 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the relation turns negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO after a prior peak signals factory slowdown and margin pressure for cyclical firms, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when HOUST is also weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if adding HOUST does not improve predictive separation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising HOUST series captures stronger residential construction demand and housing-sector activity, which tends to support cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month rises in HOUST are not followed by above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect is absent after excluding recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, policy tightness and inflation pressure jointly compress equity multiples and raise discount rates. That combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with elevated CPIAUCSL do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears once inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS indicates resilient labor demand and income growth, which supports consumption and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment is low but payroll growth stalls.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment, rising-payroll periods do not outperform on 60-day forward returns, or if low UNRATE without PAYEMS growth performs equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly rise in GDPC1 signals real output growth and improving aggregate demand, which should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal weakens if GDP growth is accompanied by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises sequentially from prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if sequential GDP gains are not associated with above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect persists even when UNRATE is rising sharply.
